old_claim.note: Portolan Charts & Maritime Navigation provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency is retained between Dry Compass Navigation and Portolan Charts & Maritime Navigation; Dry shipboard compasses are instrument hardware and do not require later portolan charts as a prerequisite.

source_shape.source_locator: Article describes 13th-century French and Italian ship-compass designs with a pivoted pointer, compass card, and protective box.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Dry shipboard compasses are instrument hardware and do not require later portolan charts as a prerequisite.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The source supports the scoped Dry Compass Navigation claim or adjacent chronology without requiring Portolan Charts & Maritime Navigation as a prerequisite.

ontology_before: Dry Compass Navigation treated Portolan Charts & Maritime Navigation as a direct prerequisite.
ontology_after: Dry Compass Navigation no longer directly depends on Portolan Charts & Maritime Navigation; the dependency boundary is narrowed to operationally necessary predecessors.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Invariant changed: dry_compass_navigation must not directly depend on portolan_charts_maritime_navigation unless a narrower source-backed node scope is introduced.
would_reject_if: A source shows dry_compass_navigation is intentionally scoped only to cases that require portolan_charts_maritime_navigation.
The graph splits dry_compass_navigation and reserves this id for a narrower technology that directly depends on portolan_charts_maritime_navigation.
A stronger source demonstrates that portolan_charts_maritime_navigation is chronologically and causally necessary for dry_compass_navigation.
short_reason: Dry shipboard compasses are instrument hardware and do not require later portolan charts as a prerequisite.
